name: TextInput
description: Input (the core single-line text field with generic leading/trailing slots, a real show/hide password toggle, a real clear button, and a separated trailing action button), TagInput (real controlled tag list — type + Enter/comma to add, click × or Backspace to remove), CounterInput (real +/- stepper with min/max/step clamping), PinInput (real multi-box OTP/PIN entry with auto-advance, backspace-back, arrow-key navigation, and paste-to-fill), and InlineInput (borderless-until-editing field with pencil → Cancel/Confirm affordance).
sourceNote: Figma's core Text Input defined 12 rigid 'types' (Basic/Email/Phone/Card/Website/Amount/Date/Search/Password/Button/Dropdown/Emoji) x 3 sizes x 6 states — 216+ variants total, each type only really differing by its leading icon and an optional trailing affordance (a shortcut badge, a password eye-toggle, a copy-link button, an inline select). Collapsed into one `Input` with generic `leading`/`trailing` slots plus a couple of named real behaviors (`showPasswordToggle`, `clearable`, `action`) — following the same promotion pattern as CheckboxCard/DropdownItem/Tag/TableCellContent. TagInput/CounterInput/PinInput/InlineInput were each their own separate Figma component (Tag Input, Counter Input, Digit Input, Inline Input) sharing the same Label/HintText composition and 3-size/6-state scheme, so all five live together under `Components/TextInput/`. Every one is genuinely functional rather than a static demo, following this project's precedent (Pagination, DatePicker, ColorPicker, Table): TagInput actually adds/removes tags, CounterInput actually increments/clamps, PinInput actually manages per-box focus and paste, InlineInput actually toggles into/out of edit mode.

action.type: { icon: string, onClick: () => void, ariaLabel?: string }
